sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game restarted after RIGHT. Blue moved right. Now let me check blue position and try a different approach - instead of BFS on adjacency, let me trace the actual path by looking at the ascii grid to understand the maze structure.
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
blue_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b']
if blue_nodes:
    print('Blue boundary:', blue_nodes[0]['boundary'])
# Let me look at the ascii to understand the maze structure. Let me look at the ascii to understand the maze structure.
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
# Find the maze area - look for non-black rows
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if 'W' in line or 'G' in line or 'b' in line or 'N' in line or 'g or 'b' in line or 'N' in line:
    if 'W' in line or 'G' in line or 'b' in line or 'N' in line:
        print(f'Row {i}: {line}')
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"world_model": "Level 1: 64x64 grid. Blue start at ~(16,16), Green target at ~(46,46). White and gray tiles form a maze-like path. Need to navigate from blue to green using UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT.", "goal_model": "Navigate from blue start to green target through the maze of white/gray tiles.", "action_model": "UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT move the blue tile.", "recent_findings": "BFS using adjacency graph didn't work. The blue tile was reset to start. The blue tile seems to have been reset to start position after game over.", "open_questions": "The game is over - need to check if there's a way to restart or if this is the end.", "plan": "Check if game can be restarted.", "cross_level_notes": ""}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
